implemented_change.summary: Drive corridor_scope under the v1 10% unknown threshold with a deterministic route-corridor source-context lane.

rule.subject: route
required_signals: subject is a true bus route, either by route descriptor or by true-route plus bus/SBS source context
route/relation/source-title context has bus, route, or corridor signal
no draft/proposed/proposal/future/will/would/could/may/candidate/potential/planned/service-plan/final-plan/redesign-plan text in the source-title/relation/endpoint context
guardrails: split-candidate and aggregate routes remain unknown
subway, rail, commuter, LIRR, MNR, and train route descriptors remain unknown
source description text is not used as a proposal veto because mixed update decks often describe observed operations and proposed follow-up work together
explicit relation lifecycle wording still runs first and can classify planned/proposed rows before this delivered-only lane

verification.result: passed: Materialized 93067/98119 submissions; SQLite FTS quick_check ok

intentionally_unresolved: Draft/proposed/service-plan/final-plan source-context route-corridor rows remain unknown unless an explicit relation lifecycle rule already classifies them.
Split-candidate, aggregate/list, subway, rail, and commuter route subjects remain unknown.
Non-route subject corridor_scope rows remain outside this lane.
Non-operates_on_corridor residuals remain outside this lane because they are below the v1 threshold once the main route-corridor family is handled.
next_candidate_lanes: Continue Plan 004 with timeline_context and claim_context, which remain above 10%.
Do not broaden corridor_scope further for v1 unless downstream consumers expose a specific residual route-timeline gap.
